answer: There are 10 possibilities for the tens place (0-9), and for each of these, there are 10 possibilities for the units place. So there are 10 x 10 = \boxed{100} three-digit numbers with at least one 5 or at least one 8 as digits. (Note: Some numbers have both a 5 and an 8 as digits, so this count is not an overestimate.)
